/pub/gfinger.help Oct 1, 2002 "gfinger" is a version of the UNIX "finger" program that lists information about user(s) on a computer. The computer that is queried must run a finger server daemon. The EECS Instructional UNIX compu

server daemon. The EECS Instructional UNIX computers use the GNU finger server, which has a specific set of options. "gfinger" command options: If you type a command such as "gfinger jdoe@cochise.cs" you get a response that includes a searc

se.cs" you get a response that includes a search of all the workstations in the "cochise" cluster. It will tell you which worksatation "jdoe" is logged into. You can use our WEB-based "clients" utility (http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/client

nts" utility (http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/clients) to locate that computer. Here are some options to the "finger" command: % gfinger .local@cory - list users on the server computer itself % gfinger .free@cory - list workstations that ha

% gfinger .free@cory - list workstations that have nobody logged in % gfinger .help@cory - list all the other options Usage: gfinger [options] name ... options: -l long listings (default for outside requests) -
listings (default for outside requests) -info long listings (GNU finger only) -face request face bitmap (GNU finger only) special names: .all list all logins .local list all local logins
.local list all local logins .help print this message .site print site information .users list all users .free list free machines .clients list al
list free machines .clients list all clients Instructional Support Group 384/386 Cory Hall, 333 Soda Hall inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
